content.content:
# Claude Code Documentation Sections

Here are all available documentation sections and their URLs:

## Getting Started
- [Overview](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/overview.md)
- [Quickstart](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/quickstart.md)
- [Common Workflows](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/common-workflows.md)
- [Claude Code on the Web](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/claude-code-on-the-web.md)

## Build with Claude Code
- [Sub-agents](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/sub-agents.md)
- [Plugins](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/plugins.md)
- [Skills](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/skills.md)
- [Output Styles](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/output-styles.md)
- [Hooks Guide](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks-guide.md)
- [Headless](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/headless.md)
- [GitHub Actions](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/github-actions.md)
- [GitLab CI/CD](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/gitlab-ci-cd.md)
- [MCP](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/mcp.md)
- [Migration Guide](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/migration-guide.md)
- [Troubleshooting](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/troubleshooting.md)

## Deployment
- [Third-Party Integrations](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/third-party-integrations.md)
- [Amazon Bedrock](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/amazon-bedrock.md)
- [Google Vertex AI](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/google-vertex-ai.md)
- [Network Config](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/network-config.md)
- [LLM Gateway](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/llm-gateway.md)
- [DevContainer](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/devcontainer.md)
- [Sandboxing](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/sandboxing.md)

## Administration
- [Setup](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/setup.md)
- [IAM](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/iam.md)
- [Security](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/security.md)
- [Data Usage](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/data-usage.md)
- [Monitoring Usage](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/monitoring-usage.md)
- [Costs](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/costs.md)
- [Analytics](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/analytics.md)
- [Plugin Marketplaces](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/plugin-marketplaces.md)

## Configuration
- [Settings](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings.md)
- [VS Code](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/vs-code.md)
- [JetBrains](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/jetbrains.md)
- [Terminal Config](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/terminal-config.md)
- [Model Config](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/model-config.md)
- [Memory](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/memory.md)
- [Statusline](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/statusline.md)

## Reference
- [CLI Reference](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/cli-reference.md)
- [Interactive Mode](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/interactive-mode.md)

- [Plugins Reference](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/plugins-reference.md)

## Resources

This integration allows Claude to analyze repositories, answer technical questions, and help developers understand and improve their codebase through simple @mentions.","url":"https://github.com/claude-did-this/claude-hub"},{"title":"Claude GitHub Webhook | Claude Did This","url":"https://claude-did-this.com/claude-hub/overview"},{"title":"GitHub - hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code: A curated list of awesome commands, files, and workflows for Claude Code","url":"https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code"},{"title":"How I’m Using Claude Code Hooks (Newest Feature) To Fully Automate My Workflow","url":"https://medium.com/@joe.njenga/use-claude-code-hooks-newest-feature-to-fully-automate-your-workflow-341b9400cfbe"},{"title":"Webhook and Claude: Automate Workflows with n8n","url":"https://n8n.io/integrations/webhook/and/claude/"},{"title":"From Passive Responses to Autonomous Execution: How Claude Code + Webhooks Transform Your Workflow Automation | by JIN | T3CH | Nov, 2025 | Medium","url":"https://medium.com/h7w/from-passive-responses-to-autonomous-execution-how-claude-code-webhooks-transform-your-workflow-d1b157337a36"},{"title":"How to integrate Webhook / API Integration & Anthropic (Claude) | 1 click ▶️ integrations","url":"https://integrately.com/integrations/anthropic/webhook-api"},{"title":"Webhooks by Zapier Anthropic (Claude) Integration - Quick Connect - Zapier","url":"https://zapier.com/apps/webhook/integrations/anthropic-claude"},{"title":"Shipping Fast(er): How I Used Claude Code to Release Chrome Webhook Extension 2.0 – Thibault Milan","url":"https://thibaultmilan.com/blog/2025/06/28/shipping-faster-how-i-used-claude-code-to-release-chrome-webhook-extension-20/"},{"title":"GitHub - disler/claude-code-hooks-mastery","url":"https://github.com/disler/claude-code-hooks-mastery"}]

Based on my search results, here's what I found about Claude Code webhooks, integrations, and new features in 2025:

## Claude Code Webhook Integrations

### Claude Hub - GitHub Integration
A webhook service that connects Claude Code to GitHub repositories, enabling AI-powered code assistance directly through pull requests and issues. This integration allows Claude to analyze repositories, answer technical questions, and help developers understand and improve their codebase through simple @mentions.

Key features include:
- Complete Feature Implementation: Claude codes entire features from requirements to deployment. Intelligent PR Management: Automatically creates, reviews, and merges pull requests. CI/CD Integration: Waits for builds, responds to test failures, and handles automated workflows. Long-running Tasks: Operates autonomously for hours until complex projects are completed.

- Claude can implement complete features, review code, merge PRs, wait for CI builds, and run for hours autonomously until tasks are completed.

### Claude Code Hooks (New Feature - July 2025)
Claude Code hooks are user-defined shell commands that execute at various points in Claude Code's lifecycle. This feature provides control over Claude Code's behavior, ensuring certain actions always happen rather than relying on the LLM to choose to run them.(no content)This captures all 8 Claude Code hook lifecycle events with their JSON payloads, including: UserPromptSubmit that fires immediately when user submits a prompt (before Claude processes it), PreToolUse that fires before any tool execution, and PostToolUse that fires after successful tool completion.

### Official GitHub Actions Integration
Claude Code GitHub Actions by Anthropic is the official GitHub Actions integration for Claude Code with examples and documentation for automating AI-powered workflows in CI/CD pipelines.

### Third-Party Automation Platforms
- Zapier seamlessly connects Webhooks by Zapier and Anthropic (Claude), automating your workflow.
- Yes, Webhook can connect with Claude using n8n.io. With n8n, you can create workflows that automate tasks and transfer data between Webhook and Claude.

### Workflow Automation Perspective
The marriage of Claude Code and webhooks unlocks something fundamentally different: an AI that doesn't wait for your input. Instead, it listens to events in your systems, and when something triggers, it springs into action.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Links: [{"title":"Claude Developer Platform | Claude","url":"https://www.claude.com/platform/api"},{"title":"A Comprehensive Guide to the Claude Code SDK","url":"https://apidog.com/blog/a-comprehensive-guide-to-the-claude-code-sdk/"},{"title":"Agent SDK overview - Claude Docs","url":"https://docs.claude.com/en/api/agent-sdk/overview"},{"title":"GitHub - codingworkflow/claude-code-api","url":"https://github.com/codingworkflow/claude-code-api/"},{"title":"Claude Developer Platform - Claude Docs","url":"https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/release-notes/overview"},{"title":"Claude API Integration Guide 2025: Complete Developer Tutorial with Code Examples - Collabnix","url":"https://collabnix.com/claude-api-integration-guide-2025-complete-developer-tutorial-with-code-examples/"},{"title":"Claude API: How to get a key and use the API","url":"https://zapier.com/blog/claude-api/"},{"title":"How to Use Claude API Key for Free in 2025","url":"https://apidog.com/blog/how-to-use-claude-api-key-for-free-in-2025/"},{"title":"Claude, Claude API, and Claude Code: What's the Difference?","url":"https://eval.16x.engineer/blog/claude-vs-claude-api-vs-claude-code"},{"title":"How to use Claude Code on the Web","url":"https://apidog.com/blog/claude-code-web/"}]

Here are the search results for "Claude Code API endpoints web SDK 2025":

## Claude Code SDK and API Information

### Claude Code SDK Overview
The Claude Code SDK (Software Development Kit) acts as a bridge, allowing developers to programmatically interact with the Claude Code model.(no content)Anthropic has released the Claude Code SDK to empower developers to seamlessly integrate this cutting-edge technology into their own applications and workflows.(no content)Instead of being confined to a web interface, developers can leverage the SDK to build custom applications, scripts, and integrations that harness the full potential of Claude Code. Whether you want to create a personalized coding assistant, automate code reviews in your CI/CD pipeline, or build a tool that can translate code from one language to another, the Claude Code SDK provides the necessary tools and interfaces.

### Agent SDK Features
The Agent SDK provides a rich tool ecosystem including file operations, code execution, web search, and MCP extensibility, along with advanced permissions for fine-grained control over agent capabilities, production essentials like built-in error handling, session management, and monitoring, and optimized Claude integration with automatic prompt caching and performance optimizations.(no content)You can extend your agents with custom tools and integrations through MCP servers, which allows you to connect to databases, APIs, and other external services.

### SDK Programmatic Access
Beside using Claude Code directly via terminal UI (TUI) as a user, Claude Code also offers programmatic access via its SDKs or a CLI tool. This means that tools like Cline, Repo Prompt, and Zed can integrate with Claude Code.

### OpenAI-Compatible API Gateway
There's also an open-source project that provides an OpenAI-compatible API for Claude Code:
Claude-code-api supports exactly the 4 Claude models that Claude Code CLI offers and provides OpenAI compatible drop-in replacement for OpenAI API endpoints. It's a simple, focused OpenAI-compatible API gateway for Claude Code with streaming support.

### Claude Developer Platform Updates (2025)
Anthropic has launched the Files API in public beta, enabling you to upload files and reference them in the Messages API and code execution tool. They've also launched the Code execution tool in public beta, which enables Claude to execute Python code in a secure, sandboxed environment, as well as the MCP connector in public beta, which allows you to connect to remote MCP servers directly from the Messages API.(no content)The Go SDK has moved from beta to GA.(no content)Claude Opus 4.5 has been launched as their most intelligent model combining maximum capability with practical performance, ideal for complex specialized tasks, professional software engineering, and advanced agents.

### Authentication
For basic authentication, retrieve a Claude API key from the Claude Console and set the ANTHROPIC_API_KEY environment variable.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Links: [{"title":"Agent SDK overview - Claude Docs","url":"https://docs.claude.com/en/api/agent-sdk/overview"},{"title":"Building agents with the Claude Agent SDK \\ Anthropic","url":"https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/building-agents-with-the-claude-agent-sdk"},{"title":"Claude Agent SDK Tutorial: Create Agents Using Claude Sonnet 4.5 | DataCamp","url":"https://www.datacamp.com/tutorial/how-to-use-claude-agent-sdk"},{"title":"How to Create Agents with Claude Agents SDK – Bind AI IDE","url":"https://blog.getbind.co/2025/10/03/how-to-create-agents-with-claude-agents-sdk/"},{"title":"Building Agents with Claude Code's SDK","url":"https://blog.promptlayer.com/building-agents-with-claude-codes-sdk/"},{"title":"Introducing Claude Agent in JetBrains IDEs | The JetBrains AI Blog","url":"https://blog.jetbrains.com/ai/2025/09/introducing-claude-agent-in-jetbrains-ides/"},{"title":"GitHub - anthropics/claude-agent-sdk-python","url":"https://github.com/anthropics/claude-agent-sdk-python"},{"title":"Introducing Claude Sonnet 4.5 \\ Anthropic","url":"https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-sonnet-4-5"},{"title":"Migrate to Claude Agent SDK - Claude Docs","url":"https://docs.claude.com/en/docs/claude-code/sdk/migration-guide"},{"title":"Enabling Claude Code to work more autonomously \\ Anthropic","url":"https://www.anthropic.com/news/enabling-claude-code-to-work-more-autonomously"}]

Here's what I found about the Claude Agent SDK documentation and features in 2025:

## Overview

Anthropic is giving developers the building blocks they use themselves to make Claude Code, called the Claude Agent SDK.(no content)The Claude Code SDK has been renamed to the Claude Agent SDK.(no content)Built on top of the agent harness that powers Claude Code, the Claude Agent SDK provides all the building blocks needed to build production-ready agents.

## Key Features

### Core Capabilities
The SDK includes:
- **Context Management**: Automatic compaction and context management to ensure your agent doesn't run out of context
- **Rich tool ecosystem**: File operations, code execution, web search, and MCP extensibility
- **Advanced permissions**: Fine-grained control over agent capabilities
- **Production essentials**: Built-in error handling, session management, and monitoring
- **Optimized Claude integration**: Automatic prompt caching and performance optimizations

### SDK Configuration Features
The SDK provides access to default features available in Claude Code, including:
- **Subagents**: Launch specialized agents stored as Markdown files in `./.claude/agents/`
- **Agent Skills**: Extend Claude with capabilities stored as SKILL.md files in `./.claude/skills/`
- **Hooks**: Execute custom commands configured in `./.claude/settings.json` that respond to tool events
- **Slash Commands**: Use custom commands defined as Markdown files in `./.claude/commands/`
- **Plugins**: Load custom plugins programmatically using the plugins option

### Language Support
The SDK offers official TypeScript/Node and Python SDKs, with streaming vs single-call modes, plus examples and demos on GitHub.(no content)The Claude Code CLI is automatically bundled with the package - no separate installation required. The SDK will use the bundled CLI by default.

### Built-in Tools
The SDK includes built-in tools (file ops, Bash, web search), plus the Model Context Protocol (MCP) to attach custom external tools.

### Custom Tools
Unlike the basic query function, ClaudeSDKClient additionally enables custom tools and hooks, both of which can be defined as Python functions. Custom tools are implemented as in-process MCP servers that run directly within your Python application, eliminating the need for separate processes.

## Agent Loop Pattern
The Claude Agent SDK outlines a structured agent loop involving four key steps: gathering context, taking action, verifying work, and repeating. In practice, start with agentic search for context and layer in semantic search when needed for speed. Actions should center on high-signal tools, bash scripts, and integrations with external services via the Model Context Protocol. Verification combines rules-based checks, visual feedback, and optional LLM assistance.

## IDE Integration
The Claude Agent is now seamlessly integrated into JetBrains IDEs via the AI chat and included in the JetBrains AI subscription. No extra plugins, no extra subscriptions – just advanced AI coding assistance built on Anthropic's Agent SDK.

## Use Cases
Deep research agents can be built that conduct comprehensive research across large document collections by searching through file systems, analyzing and synthesizing information from multiple sources, cross-referencing data across files, and generating detailed reports.(no content)Developers are already building agents for a broad range of use cases with the SDK, including financial compliance agents, cybersecurity agents, and code debugging agents.

results:
Here's what I found about the Claude Agent SDK documentation and features in 2025:

## Overview

Anthropic is giving developers the building blocks they use themselves to make Claude Code, called the Claude Agent SDK.(no content)The Claude Code SDK has been renamed to the Claude Agent SDK.(no content)Built on top of the agent harness that powers Claude Code, the Claude Agent SDK provides all the building blocks needed to build production-ready agents.

## Key Features

### Core Capabilities
The SDK includes:
- **Context Management**: Automatic compaction and context management to ensure your agent doesn't run out of context
- **Rich tool ecosystem**: File operations, code execution, web search, and MCP extensibility
- **Advanced permissions**: Fine-grained control over agent capabilities
- **Production essentials**: Built-in error handling, session management, and monitoring
- **Optimized Claude integration**: Automatic prompt caching and performance optimizations

### SDK Configuration Features
The SDK provides access to default features available in Claude Code, including:
- **Subagents**: Launch specialized agents stored as Markdown files in `./.claude/agents/`
- **Agent Skills**: Extend Claude with capabilities stored as SKILL.md files in `./.claude/skills/`
- **Hooks**: Execute custom commands configured in `./.claude/settings.json` that respond to tool events
- **Slash Commands**: Use custom commands defined as Markdown files in `./.claude/commands/`
- **Plugins**: Load custom plugins programmatically using the plugins option

### Language Support
The SDK offers official TypeScript/Node and Python SDKs, with streaming vs single-call modes, plus examples and demos on GitHub.(no content)The Claude Code CLI is automatically bundled with the package - no separate installation required. The SDK will use the bundled CLI by default.

### Built-in Tools
The SDK includes built-in tools (file ops, Bash, web search), plus the Model Context Protocol (MCP) to attach custom external tools.

### Custom Tools
Unlike the basic query function, ClaudeSDKClient additionally enables custom tools and hooks, both of which can be defined as Python functions. Custom tools are implemented as in-process MCP servers that run directly within your Python application, eliminating the need for separate processes.

## Agent Loop Pattern
The Claude Agent SDK outlines a structured agent loop involving four key steps: gathering context, taking action, verifying work, and repeating. In practice, start with agentic search for context and layer in semantic search when needed for speed. Actions should center on high-signal tools, bash scripts, and integrations with external services via the Model Context Protocol. Verification combines rules-based checks, visual feedback, and optional LLM assistance.

## IDE Integration
The Claude Agent is now seamlessly integrated into JetBrains IDEs via the AI chat and included in the JetBrains AI subscription. No extra plugins, no extra subscriptions – just advanced AI coding assistance built on Anthropic's Agent SDK.

## Use Cases
Deep research agents can be built that conduct comprehensive research across large document collections by searching through file systems, analyzing and synthesizing information from multiple sources, cross-referencing data across files, and generating detailed reports.(no content)Developers are already building agents for a broad range of use cases with the SDK, including financial compliance agents, cybersecurity agents, and code debugging agents.

What are the capabilities and features of Claude Code on the Web including API endpoints and integration methods?
What is the headless mode and how can it be used for programmatic access?
What are the MCP capabilities and how to install and configure MCP servers?

content.content:
# Claude Code on the Web: Capabilities and Features

## Core Capabilities

Claude Code on the web enables developers to execute coding tasks asynchronously on secure cloud infrastructure. The platform excels at "answering questions" about code architecture, handling "bugfixes and routine tasks," and managing "parallel work" across multiple assignments.

## Key Features

**Task Execution:**
The service supports repository cloning, environment setup, code analysis, automated testing, and change management—all culminating in pull request creation through GitHub.

**Flexible Workflow:**
Users can initiate tasks via the web interface and seamlessly transition to local terminal work using the "Open in CLI" button, allowing continued development locally.

**Pre-configured Environment:**
The universal image includes popular languages (Python, Node.js, Ruby, PHP, Java, Go, Rust, C++), databases (PostgreSQL, Redis), and common development toolchains without requiring manual setup.

## Integration & Access

Claude Code on the web connects exclusively with GitHub repositories through a dedicated proxy service. The platform manages authentication securely using scoped credentials rather than exposing actual tokens within the sandbox.

**Availability:**
The research preview is limited to Pro users, Max users, and premium Team/Enterprise seat holders.

## Notable Limitation

The platform currently only supports GitHub-hosted repositories—GitLab and other version control systems are incompatible with cloud-based sessions.

## Notable Limitation

content.content:
# Headless Mode Overview

Headless mode enables running Claude Code programmatically through command-line scripts without an interactive interface. This allows automation tools and CI/CD pipelines to leverage Claude's capabilities.

## Key Features

**Primary Usage:** The `--print` flag activates non-interactive execution. As the documentation states, this mode "allows you to run Claude Code programmatically from command line scripts and automation tools without any interactive UI."

**Configuration:** You can control tool access via `--allowedTools` and `--disallowedTools`, manage conversation sessions with `--resume` or `--continue`, and customize behavior with system prompts using `--append-system-prompt`.

## Output & Input Options

The tool supports multiple formats:
- **Text output** (default) returns plain responses
- **JSON output** provides structured data with metadata like cost and execution time
- **Streaming JSON** emits messages incrementally as they arrive

For multi-turn conversations, you can persist context by resuming sessions with their IDs, enabling complex workflows across multiple prompts without relaunching the application.

## Practical Applications

The documentation provides three integration examples: an automated incident response system for SRE teams, security audit automation for pull requests, and a multi-turn legal document review assistant—all demonstrating how to build intelligent agents with session persistence and controlled tool access.

content.content:
# MCP Capabilities and Installation Guide

## MCP Capabilities

Claude Code connects to external tools through the Model Context Protocol (MCP), an open standard for AI-tool integrations. With MCP servers enabled, you can:

- **Automate workflows**: "Create Gmail drafts inviting users to feedback sessions"
- **Access external data**: Query databases, retrieve design files, and pull monitoring information
- **Integrate services**: Connect to issue trackers, APIs, databases, and cloud platforms
- **Reference resources**: Use @ mentions to attach MCP-exposed resources to prompts
- **Execute custom commands**: Trigger MCP prompts as slash commands (e.g., `/mcp__github__list_prs`)

## Installation Methods

MCP servers can be installed three ways:

**1. Remote HTTP servers** (recommended for cloud services)
```bash
claude mcp add --transport http notion https://mcp.notion.com/mcp
```

**2. Remote SSE servers** (deprecated; use HTTP instead)
```bash
claude mcp add --transport sse asana https://mcp.asana.com/sse
```

**3. Local stdio servers** (for processes requiring system access)
```bash
claude mcp add --transport stdio airtable -- npx -y airtable-mcp-server
```

## Configuration Management

Key commands include listing servers (`claude mcp list`), retrieving details (`claude mcp get`), and removing servers (`claude mcp remove`). Use scopes to control accessibility: **local** (project-only), **project** (team-shared via `.mcp.json`), or **user** (cross-project access).

Authentication uses OAuth 2.0; invoke `/mcp` within Claude Code to authenticate with remote servers requiring credentials.

- **Integrate services**: Connect to issue trackers, APIs, databases, and cloud platforms

- **Execute custom commands**: Trigger MCP prompts as slash commands (e.g., `/mcp__github__list_prs`)

## Installation Methods

MCP servers can be installed three ways:

**1. Remote HTTP servers** (recommended for cloud services)
```bash
claude mcp add --transport http notion https://mcp.notion.com/mcp
```

**2. Remote SSE servers** (deprecated; use HTTP instead)
```bash
claude mcp add --transport sse asana https://mcp.asana.com/sse
```

**3. Local stdio servers** (for processes requiring system access)
```bash
claude mcp add --transport stdio airtable -- npx -y airtable-mcp-server
```

## Configuration Management

Key commands include listing servers (`claude mcp list`), retrieving details (`claude mcp get`), and removing servers (`claude mcp remove`). Use scopes to control accessibility: **local** (project-only), **project** (team-shared via `.mcp.json`), or **user** (cross-project access).

Authentication uses OAuth 2.0; invoke `/mcp` within Claude Code to authenticate with remote servers requiring credentials.

input.prompt: What are the latest releases and updates for Claude API and tools?

content.content:
# Latest Claude API & Tools Updates

The most recent updates include:

**November 24, 2025:**
- **Claude Opus 4.5** launched as "our most intelligent model combining maximum capability with practical performance," featuring major improvements in vision, coding, and computer use.
- **Programmatic tool calling** (beta) reduces latency and token usage in multi-tool workflows
- **Tool search tool** (beta) enables dynamic tool discovery from large catalogs
- **Effort parameter** (beta) for Claude Opus 4.5 allows trading response thoroughness for efficiency
- **Client-side compaction** added to Python/TypeScript SDKs for automatic context management

**November 19, 2025:**
New documentation platform launched at platform.claude.com/docs, consolidating resources with the Claude Console for a unified developer experience.

**November 18, 2025:**
Claude now available in Microsoft Foundry with Azure billing, OAuth authentication, and support for extended thinking, prompt caching, Files API, and Agent Skills.

**November 14, 2025:**
**Structured outputs** (beta) provides guaranteed schema conformance, available for Claude Sonnet 4.5 and Opus 4.1.

These updates emphasize enhanced capability, developer tools, and enterprise integrations across the platform.

Based on the search results, here's information about Claude's Messages API, Files API, and Code Execution Tool in 2025:

## Code Execution Tool

The code execution tool is currently in public beta. To use this feature, add the "code-execution-2025-08-25" beta header to your API requests.(no content)The current version code_execution_20250825 supports Bash commands and file operations. A legacy version code_execution_20250522 (Python only) is also available.(no content)By upgrading to code-execution-2025-08-25, you get access to file manipulation and Bash capabilities, including code in multiple languages.

### Key Capabilities:
File operations: Create, view, and edit files directly, including writing code. Claude can use any combination of these capabilities in a single request. All operations run in a secure sandbox environment.

### Pricing:
Each organization receives 50 free hours of usage with the code execution tool per day. Additional usage beyond the first 50 hours is billed at $0.05 per hour, per container.(no content)Execution time has a minimum of 5 minutes. If files are included in the request, execution time is billed even if the tool is not used due to files being preloaded onto the container.

## Files API

The Files API lets you upload and manage files to use with the Claude API without re-uploading content with each request. This is particularly useful when using the code execution tool to provide inputs (e.g. datasets and documents) and then download outputs (e.g. charts).(no content)The Files API is currently in beta.(no content)To use the Files API, you'll need to include the beta feature header: anthropic-beta: files-api-2025-04-14.

### Using Files API with Code Execution:
Using the Files API with Code Execution requires two beta headers: "anthropic-beta": "code-execution-2025-08-25,files-api-2025-04-14"(no content)Images are supported in all Claude 3+ models, PDFs in all Claude 3.5+ models, and various other file types for the code execution tool in Claude 3.5 Haiku plus all Claude 3.7+ models.(no content)You can only download files created by skills or the code execution tool.

## Agent Skills (New Feature)

Anthropic has launched Agent Skills (skills-2025-10-02 beta), a new way to extend Claude's capabilities. Skills are organized folders of instructions, scripts, and resources that Claude loads dynamically to perform specialized tasks.(no content)Skills require the Code Execution Tool beta, which provides the secure environment they need to run.(no content)Use Anthropic-created skills to have Claude read and generate professional Excel spreadsheets with formulas, PowerPoint presentations, Word documents, and fillable PDFs.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Based on the search results, here's comprehensive information about Claude MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers available in 2025:

## Overview

The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is an open standard, open-source framework introduced by Anthropic in November 2024 to standardize the way AI systems like large language models integrate and share data with external tools, systems, and data sources.(no content)Often described as the "USB-C for AI," MCP servers act as bridges, enabling AI agents to perform tasks ranging from 3D modeling to web automation and database querying.

## Ecosystem Scale

According to the MCP servers' GitHub rankings, MCP.so offers a comprehensive server index with over 3,000 MCP servers and quality ratings, while Smithery lists over 2,200 servers with automated installation guides.

## Major Adopters

In March 2025, OpenAI officially adopted the MCP, following a decision to integrate the standard across its products, including the ChatGPT desktop app, OpenAI's Agents SDK, and the Responses API.(no content)The protocol's rapid uptake by OpenAI, Google DeepMind, and toolmakers like Zed and Sourcegraph suggests growing consensus around its utility.

## Popular MCP Servers Available

### Pre-built Official Servers
Anthropic provides pre-built MCP servers for popular enterprise systems like Google Drive, Slack, GitHub, Git, Postgres, and Puppeteer.

### Top MCP Servers for Developers (2025)
Some of the best Claude MCP servers include GitHub, Filesystem, Desktop Commander, Notion, Supabase, and Brave Search, depending on whether you're coding, researching, or writing.

### Categorized List of Servers:

**Version Control & Development:**
- GitHub MCP Server – Complete repository management, including commits, branches, PRs, and issues directly from Claude conversations.
- GitLab MCP Server – Built for GitLab Premium and Ultimate users, enables AI-powered CI/CD pipeline inspection, merge request management, and secure code review. Uses OAuth 2.0 authentication and is ideal for enterprise teams with self-hosted GitLab environments.
- Git MCP Server – A Model Context Protocol server for Git repository interaction and automation, providing tools to read, search, and manipulate Git repositories via Large Language Models.

**Productivity & Communication:**
- Slack MCP Server – Automate team communication: draft replies, summarize threads, search channels, and trigger workflow notifications.
- Google Drive MCP Server – Search, summarize, and organize cloud documents across personal and shared team drives seamlessly.
- Discord MCP Server – Enable AI assistants to seamlessly interact with Discord, managing channels, messages, users, and webhooks.

**Database & Analytics:**
- ClickHouse – Query your ClickHouse database server.
- GreptimeDB MCP Server is engineered for secure, real-time database interaction through AI agents, making it ideal for metrics, logging, and time-series analytics.
- Chroma MCP Server is compatible with Python 3.8+, supports both ephemeral and persistent client types, and offers HTTP and cloud deployment options with robust APIs for CRUD operations and semantic search capabilities.

**Financial Data:**
- Octagon MCP Server offers deep financial data access powered by specialized AI agents for public filings, transcripts, market data, and private company profiles.

**System & Desktop:**
- Desktop Commander – Execute terminal commands, launch applications, automate file operations, and control system-level desktop functions.

**Web & Browser Automation:**
- Browser-use MCP server allows AI agents to control web browsers, enabling automated web interactions and tasks, providing a seamless interface for AI to navigate, manipulate, and extract data from web pages efficiently.

**Integration Platforms:**
- Rube is a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that connects your AI tools to 500+ apps like Gmail, Slack, GitHub, and Notion. Simply install it in your AI client, authenticate once with your apps, and start asking your AI to perform real actions.

## Compatibility

All MCP servers work with Claude Desktop through local configuration or one-click desktop extensions (.mcpb files). Claude on the web (claude.ai) requires a paid plan (Pro, Max, Team, or Enterprise) to connect to remote MCP servers through the Integrations feature.

## Security Considerations

In April 2025, security researchers released analysis showing multiple outstanding security issues with MCP, including prompt injection, tool permissions where combining tools can exfiltrate files, and lookalike tools that can silently replace trusted ones.

Based on the search results, here's comprehensive information about Claude MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers available in 2025:

## Overview

The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is an open standard, open-source framework introduced by Anthropic in November 2024 to standardize the way AI systems like large language models integrate and share data with external tools, systems, and data sources.(no content)Often described as the "USB-C for AI," MCP servers act as bridges, enabling AI agents to perform tasks ranging from 3D modeling to web automation and database querying.

## Ecosystem Scale

According to the MCP servers' GitHub rankings, MCP.so offers a comprehensive server index with over 3,000 MCP servers and quality ratings, while Smithery lists over 2,200 servers with automated installation guides.

## Major Adopters

In March 2025, OpenAI officially adopted the MCP, following a decision to integrate the standard across its products, including the ChatGPT desktop app, OpenAI's Agents SDK, and the Responses API.(no content)The protocol's rapid uptake by OpenAI, Google DeepMind, and toolmakers like Zed and Sourcegraph suggests growing consensus around its utility.

## Popular MCP Servers Available

### Pre-built Official Servers
Anthropic provides pre-built MCP servers for popular enterprise systems like Google Drive, Slack, GitHub, Git, Postgres, and Puppeteer.

### Top MCP Servers for Developers (2025)
Some of the best Claude MCP servers include GitHub, Filesystem, Desktop Commander, Notion, Supabase, and Brave Search, depending on whether you're coding, researching, or writing.

### Categorized List of Servers:

**Version Control & Development:**
- GitHub MCP Server – Complete repository management, including commits, branches, PRs, and issues directly from Claude conversations.
- GitLab MCP Server – Built for GitLab Premium and Ultimate users, enables AI-powered CI/CD pipeline inspection, merge request management, and secure code review. Uses OAuth 2.0 authentication and is ideal for enterprise teams with self-hosted GitLab environments.
- Git MCP Server – A Model Context Protocol server for Git repository interaction and automation, providing tools to read, search, and manipulate Git repositories via Large Language Models.

**Productivity & Communication:**
- Slack MCP Server – Automate team communication: draft replies, summarize threads, search channels, and trigger workflow notifications.
- Google Drive MCP Server – Search, summarize, and organize cloud documents across personal and shared team drives seamlessly.
- Discord MCP Server – Enable AI assistants to seamlessly interact with Discord, managing channels, messages, users, and webhooks.

**Database & Analytics:**
- ClickHouse – Query your ClickHouse database server.
- GreptimeDB MCP Server is engineered for secure, real-time database interaction through AI agents, making it ideal for metrics, logging, and time-series analytics.
- Chroma MCP Server is compatible with Python 3.8+, supports both ephemeral and persistent client types, and offers HTTP and cloud deployment options with robust APIs for CRUD operations and semantic search capabilities.

**Financial Data:**
- Octagon MCP Server offers deep financial data access powered by specialized AI agents for public filings, transcripts, market data, and private company profiles.

**System & Desktop:**
- Desktop Commander – Execute terminal commands, launch applications, automate file operations, and control system-level desktop functions.

**Web & Browser Automation:**
- Browser-use MCP server allows AI agents to control web browsers, enabling automated web interactions and tasks, providing a seamless interface for AI to navigate, manipulate, and extract data from web pages efficiently.

**Integration Platforms:**
- Rube is a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that connects your AI tools to 500+ apps like Gmail, Slack, GitHub, and Notion. Simply install it in your AI client, authenticate once with your apps, and start asking your AI to perform real actions.

## Compatibility

All MCP servers work with Claude Desktop through local configuration or one-click desktop extensions (.mcpb files). Claude on the web (claude.ai) requires a paid plan (Pro, Max, Team, or Enterprise) to connect to remote MCP servers through the Integrations feature.

## Security Considerations

In April 2025, security researchers released analysis showing multiple outstanding security issues with MCP, including prompt injection, tool permissions where combining tools can exfiltrate files, and lookalike tools that can silently replace trusted ones.

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have comprehensive information. Let me provide a detailed summary to the user:

## Comprehensive Summary: Claude Code Web API, SDK, and Latest Features

Based on my research of official documentation and the latest updates, here's a comprehensive overview of what's available:

---

### 1. Claude Agent SDK (Renamed from Claude Code SDK)

The **Claude Code SDK has been officially renamed to the Claude Agent SDK** and represents Anthropic's core building blocks for creating production-ready agents.

**Language Support:**
- Official TypeScript/Node SDK
- Official Python SDK
- The Claude Code CLI is automatically bundled (no separate installation)

**Core Capabilities:**
- **Context Management**: Automatic context compaction to prevent overflow
- **Rich Tool Ecosystem**: File operations, code execution, web search, and MCP extensibility
- **Advanced Permissions**: Fine-grained control over agent capabilities
- **Production Features**: Built-in error handling, session management, and monitoring
- **Performance Optimization**: Automatic prompt caching and optimizations

**Configuration Features:**
- Subagents (launch specialized agents from `./.claude/agents/`)
- Agent Skills (extend capabilities with `./.claude/skills/`)
- Hooks (execute custom commands responding to tool events)
- Slash Commands (custom commands as Markdown files)
- Custom Plugins (load programmatically)

---

### 2. Claude Code on the Web

Claude Code on the web (available to Pro, Max, and Team/Enterprise users) provides:

**Capabilities:**
- Execute coding tasks asynchronously on secure cloud infrastructure
- Repository cloning and environment setup
- Code analysis and automated testing
- Pull request creation through GitHub
- Pre-configured environment with popular languages (Python, Node.js, Ruby, PHP, Java, Go, Rust, C++)
- Databases (PostgreSQL, Redis)

**Access Methods:**
- Web interface for task initiation
- "Open in CLI" button for local continuation
- GitHub-only integration (GitLab not yet supported)

---

### 3. Claude API: Messages API with New Tools

**Files API (Public Beta)** - `anthropic-beta: files-api-2025-04-14`
- Upload and manage files for reuse across requests
- Eliminates need to re-upload content
- Works seamlessly with Code Execution tool
- Particularly useful for datasets, documents, and outputs

**Code Execution Tool (Public Beta)** - `anthropic-beta: code-execution-2025-08-25`
- Current version: `code_execution_20250825` (Bash and file operations)
- Legacy version: `code_execution_20250522` (Python only)
- Secure sandboxed environment
- File manipulation and Bash capabilities

**Pricing for Code Execution:**
- 50 free hours per day per organization
- Additional usage: $0.05/hour per container
- Minimum billing: 5 minutes (with file uploads, billing occurs even if tool unused)

**Agent Skills (New Feature)** - `skills-2025-10-02` (beta)
- Organized folders of instructions, scripts, and resources
- Dynamically loaded capabilities
- Includes pre-built skills for Excel, PowerPoint, Word documents, and fillable PDFs
- Requires Code Execution Tool

---

### 4. Headless Mode (Programmatic Access)

Claude Code can be run programmatically without interactive UI:

**Key Features:**
- `--print` flag for non-interactive execution
- `--allowedTools` and `--disallowedTools` for access control
- `--resume` and `--continue` for session management
- `--append-system-prompt` for customization

**Output Options:**
- Plain text (default)
- JSON (with cost and execution time metadata)
- Streaming JSON (incremental updates)

**Applications:**
- Automated incident response systems
- Security audit automation
- Multi-turn document review assistants

---

### 5. Hooks (New Feature - July 2025)

User-defined shell commands that execute at various lifecycle points:

**Lifecycle Events:**
- `UserPromptSubmit`: Fires when user submits a prompt (before processing)
- `PreToolUse`: Fires before any tool execution
- `PostToolUse`: Fires after successful tool completion
- Plus 5 additional lifecycle events

**Configuration:** Defined in `./.claude/settings.json`

---

### 6. Model Context Protocol (MCP)

MCP is an open standard for integrating external tools and data sources (described as "USB-C for AI").

**Installation Methods:**
```bash
# Remote HTTP servers (recommended)
claude mcp add --transport http notion https://mcp.notion.com/mcp

# Local stdio servers
claude mcp add --transport stdio airtable -- npx -y airtable-mcp-server
```

**Key Management Commands:**
- `claude mcp list` - List all servers
- `claude mcp get <name>` - Get server details
- `claude mcp remove <name>` - Remove servers

**Popular MCP Servers Available (2025):**

**Official Anthropic Servers:**
- Google Drive, Slack, GitHub, Git, Postgres, Puppeteer

**Top Community Servers:**
- **Version Control**: GitHub, GitLab, Git
- **Productivity**: Slack, Google Drive, Discord
- **Database**: ClickHouse, GreptimeDB, Chroma, Supabase
- **Finance**: Octagon (public filings, transcripts, market data)
- **System**: Desktop Commander (terminal, file operations)
- **Web Automation**: Browser-use (web control)
- **Integration Platforms**: Rube (500+ apps like Gmail, GitHub, Notion)

**Scale:** Over 3,000+ MCP servers available in the ecosystem

**Authentication:** OAuth 2.0 for remote servers (invoke `/mcp` within Claude Code)

**Scope Control:** Local, project-shared, or user-wide access

---

### 7. Official GitHub Actions Integration

**Claude Code GitHub Actions** by Anthropic is the official integration for CI/CD pipeline automation.

**Related Capabilities:**
- Claude Hub webhook service connects Claude Code to GitHub repositories
- Enables @mentions in PRs and issues
- Supports autonomous tasks (hours-long operations until completion)
- Intelligent PR management and CI/CD integration

---

### 8. Latest Claude Platform Updates (November 2025)

**Claude Opus 4.5** (Latest Released Model)
- "Most intelligent model combining maximum capability with practical performance"
- Improvements in vision, coding, and computer use
- Available on all major platforms

**New Features:**
- **Programmatic Tool Calling** (beta) - Reduces latency and token usage
- **Tool Search Tool** (beta) - Dynamic tool discovery from large catalogs
- **Effort Parameter** (beta) - Trade response thoroughness for efficiency
- **Client-side Compaction** (SDK feature) - Automatic context management
- **Structured Outputs** (beta) - Guaranteed schema conformance
- **Extended Thinking** - Advanced reasoning capability
- **Prompt Caching** - Optimize repeated requests

**Integration Updates:**
- New unified documentation platform: `platform.claude.com/docs`
- Microsoft Foundry integration with Azure billing
- OpenAI officially adopted MCP (March 2025)

---

### 9. Programmatic Access Methods

**Available Options:**
1. **Claude Agent SDK** (primary): TypeScript/Node and Python
2. **Headless CLI mode**: `--print` flag with JSON output
3. **OpenAI-Compatible Gateway**: Third-party `claude-code-api` project
4. **Messages API**: Direct API calls with streaming support
5. **MCP Servers**: Custom tool integrations

---

### Key Files in Your Configuration

You have several custom capabilities already configured:

**Custom Slash Commands:**
- `/init`, `/pr-comments`, `/statusline`, `/review`, `/security-review`

**MCP Servers:**
- `gemini-cli`
- `chrome-devtools`

---

### Recent Security Considerations (April 2025)

MCP security analysis identified several issues to be aware of:
- Prompt injection vulnerabilities
- Tool permission combinations allowing file exfiltration
- Lookalike tools that can replace trusted ones

---
